params.merkleProof: Proof of credential inclusion in the source state

params.syncKey: The key identifying the pending sync to cancel
The key identifying the pending sync

getRoleAdmin(bytes32).details: Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}.

grantRole(bytes32,address).details: Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event.

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

params.fraudProofWindow: Seconds to wait before finalizing bridge messages
params.genesisRoot: Initial state root for the light client
params.rateLimit: Maximum relays per operator per window

params.didHash: Hash of the full DID string

params.chainId: The chain where this method resolves
params.methodHash: Hash of the DID method string (e.g., keccak256("did:zeroid"))

renounceRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

params.chainId: The chain to resume bridging for

params.didHash: Hash of the DID to resolve

returns.ref: The DID reference (may require cross-chain lookup)

revokeRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

params.fraudEvidence: Proof that the message is invalid

supportsInterface(bytes4).details: Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. See the corresponding https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-165#how-interfaces-are-identified[ERC section] to learn more about how these ids are created. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas.

params.chainId: The chain to halt bridging for

params.blockNumber: Block number corresponding to the state root

addStake().notice: Add more stake as an existing operator.

bridgeCredential(bytes32,uint256,bytes,b.notice: Bridge a credential to another chain.

cancelRevocationSync(bytes32).notice: Cancel a queued revocation sync (governance only).

finalizeCredential(bytes32).notice: Finalize a bridged credential after the fraud proof window.

finalizeRevocationSync(bytes32).notice: Finalize a queued revocation sync after the delay period.

registerChain(uint256,bytes32,uint256,ui.notice: Register a new chain for bridging.

registerDID(bytes32,bytes32).notice: Register or update a DID document reference.         ZID-007: On first registration, binds the DID to msg.sender as controller.         On subsequent updates, only the original controller can update.

registerDIDMethod(bytes32,uint256).notice: Register a DID method to chain mapping.

registerOperator().notice: Register as a bridge operator by staking.

resetCircuitBreaker(uint256).notice: Reset the circuit breaker for a chain.

revocationSyncDelay().notice: Configurable delay before a revocation sync can be finalized

setRevocationSyncDelay(uint256).notice: Update the revocation sync delay (governance only).

submitFraudProof(bytes32,bytes).notice: Submit a fraud proof against a pending bridge message.         Only authorized challengers may submit. A challenge bond is required         and will be slashed if the challenge is invalid. Challenges must be         submitted within CHALLENGE_WINDOW of the message's creation.

syncRevocation((uint256,bytes32,uint256,.notice: Queue a revocation accumulator sync from another chain.         The sync must be finalized after the delay period.

tripCircuitBreaker(uint256,string).notice: Trip the circuit breaker for a chain.

updateLightClient(uint256,bytes32,uint25.notice: Update the light client state root for a chain.

withdrawStake(uint256).notice: Withdraw stake (only after lock period and if not slashed).
